arabic: الدُّعَاءُ عِبَادَةٌ عَظِيمَةٌ وَهُوَ مُخُّ الْعِبَادَةِ كَمَا قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ [رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَحَسَّنَهُ الْأَلْبَانِيُّ]. يَدُلُّ الدُّعَاءُ عَلَى افْتِقَارِ الْعَبْدِ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَاعْتِرَافِهِ بِضَعْفِهِ. وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ أَنْ يَسْأَلَهُ عِبَادُهُ وَيَكْرَهُ أَنْ يَسْتَغْنِيَ أَحَدٌ عَنْهُ. وَأَفْضَلُ أَوْقَاتِ الدُّعَاءِ آخِرُ اللَّيْلِ وَبَيْنَ الْأَذَانِ وَالْإِقَامَةِ. وَالدَّرْسُ أَنَّ الْمُسْلِمَ يَلْجَأُ إِلَى اللَّهِ بِالدُّعَاءِ فِي كُلِّ أَمْرٍ صَغِيرٍ أَوْ كَبِيرٍ.
translation: Dova je velika ibadet i srž ibadeta, kako je rekao Vjerovjesnik, sallallahu alejhi ve sellem [Bilježi Tirmizi, a dobrim ga ocijenio Albani]. Dova ukazuje na to da je rob ovisan o Allahu i da priznaje svoju slabost. Allah voli da Ga Njegovi robovi mole i mrzi da Mu se neko smatra nepotrebnim. Najbolja vremena za dovu su posljednji dio noći i između ezana i ikameta. Pouka je da musliman pribjegava Allahu dovom u svakoj stvari, maloj ili velikoj.
translationEn: Du'a (supplication) is a great act of worship and the very essence of worship, as the Prophet, peace be upon him, said [Narrated by al-Tirmidhi, graded hasan by al-Albani]. Du'a shows a servant's dependence on Allah and their acknowledgement of their weakness. Allah loves that His servants ask of Him and dislikes that anyone feels independent of Him. The best times for du'a are the last part of the night and between the adhan and iqamah. The lesson is that a Muslim turns to Allah in supplication in every matter, great or small.
